english: ba yod (ba yod) the fish rolls its golden eye Mil.; to whirl, to eddy, to move round rapidly, of the water, so prob. Dzl. 62, 13; 246, 2; krom dmar-nág °kyil-ba (khrom dmar nag 'khyil ba) the motley crowd in a whirling motion Pth.; to hang or flow down in folds, of a tent or a curtain Glr. 33, 12. — 2. to flow (whirling) together, used of rivulets and brooks overflowing so as to form small lakes Mil.; of persons: to meet, to flock or crowd together, mi mán-po dé-ru °kyil-bar gyúr-te (mi mang po de ru 'khyil bar gyur te) Pth.; *°kyil-ču*, (*'khyil chu*) and *ču-°kyil* (*chu 'khyil*) col. puddle.

pf. kyug (khyug) 1. Cs.: to run; °kyúg-po ('khyug po) a runner; °kyúg-yig ('khyug yig) running hand, current hand-writing, as is used in the writing of letters etc.; °kyúg-po °kyú-ba ('khyug po 'khyu ba) Lex. is explained by Sch.: to run away hastily. The signification of running, however, seems to be obsolete, whilst the usual meaning is: 2. to dart or sweep rapidly along, frq. used of a flash of lightning, also of the rapid motion of a fish in the water Mil.; of spectral apparitions Mil.; of acute rheumatic pains: of the light: to flash, to shoot rays of light, kra-kyug-kyúg-pa (khra khyug khyug pa) to gleam, to sparkle with light, to shine in various colours Pth.; ser-°kyúg-ge-ba (ser 'khyug ge ba) glittering in yellow lustre Mil.; to glitter, to shine, of the rainbow; to shine through, of the veins through the cuticle etc. — *°kyúg-šar-cán* (*'khyug shar can*) W. hasty, hurrying, careless.

1. to embrace frq., mgúl-nas °kyúd-pa (mgul nas 'khyud pa) to clasp round the neck, to hug; to encompass by spanning Pth. and elsewhere, cf. °kyígs-pa ('khyigs pa). — 2. to glide in or into (as serpents), mńál-du °kyúg-pa (mngal du 'khyug pa) of the soul in the new conception, like the synonym kril-ba (khril ba), for °júg-pa ('jug pa). — 3. to be able, *nád-pa mál-sa-na láń-ńa* (*nad pa mal sa na lang nga*) (instead of lań-bar (lang bar)) *mi °kyud* (*mi 'khyud*) the sick man is not able to rise from his bed. — blos mi °kyúd-pa byéd-pa (blos mi 'khyud pa byed pa) Thgy. (not clear).
Cs.: to be separated, divorced; Lex.: bskúr-bas °kyúr-ba, (bskur bas 'khyur ba,) therefore prob. the vb. n. to skyúr-ba, (skyur ba,) to be deserted, cast off.
seems to be = géns-pa (gengs pa) C. col.

(1. cf. gyed-pa (gyed pa)) Cs. to be distributed, e.g. food, Dzl. — 2. C. and W. to be sufficient, to suffice, to be enough, to hold out, *mi °kyed* (*mi 'khyed*) there is not enough. — 3. C. to gain (a law-suit), to be acquitted. — 4. pyir °kyéd-pa (phyir 'khyed pa) to bow without uncovering one's head, as a less humble way of saluting Mil.nt.

pf. °Kyer ('Khyer) (Northern Ld. *Kyers* (*Khyers*)), at the end of a sentence kyér-ro (khyer ro) Tar. and others, (Cs. kyer-to? (khyer to?)), nearly the same as °kur-ba ('khur ba); (the नय of the Lexx.: to lead, to guide, does not fully agree with the sense in which it is generally used) — 1. to carry away, to take away; čus (chus) to be carried or swept away by water; fig.: lé-los (le los) to be overcome, carried away by idleness Mil.; ldé-mig kyer (lde mig khyer) take the key with you! — 2. to carry, to bring, in a more general sense, C. and B.: kyer šog (khyer shog) bring! kyer soń (khyer song) carry off! take away! (in a like manner as °kúr-ba ('khur ba)); des ču bláń-nas kyer °óń-bai lám-ka-na (des chu blangs nas khyer 'ong ba'i lam kha na) he having fetched water, being on his way to bring it Pth.; kyer-la šog (khyer la shog) bring me (word), let me know (the result of your investigation) Mil. — °kyér-so ('khyer so) 1. appearance, esp. a neat, handsome appearance of persons or things. 2. advantage, superiority, pleasantness, Mil., C.
pf. kyag (khyag), imp. kyog (khyog), to lift, lift up, = °tógs-pa ('thogs pa), tégs-pa (thegs pa) Glr.; to carry, bring, *sól-ja kyog* (*sol ja khyog*) bring in the tea C., cf. sub kag (khag).

or kyóg-po (khyog po) crooked, bent; kyóg-poi ri-mo (khyog po'i ri mo) a crooked figure, a curve, flourish, crescent etc.; nyas pár kyog tsur kyog byás-šiń (nyas phar khyog tshur khyog byas shing) the fish winding its body, writhing Pth.; °kyog-cán ('khyog can), °kyog-kyóg ('khyog khyog) tortuous; °kyog-bšád ('khyog bshad) a crooked, out of the way construction or explanation. — °kyog stón-pa ('khyog ston pa) to fly into a passion (?) Sch.
